title: silene conica subsp. sartorii
intro: Silene conica subsp. sartorii, also known as the Alpine Catchfly, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Silene conica subsp. sartorii include its ability to soothe sore throats and ease congestion, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
